finding: Fix E: wireWindowRecovery button label comparison broken by localization. `handle()` checks `button === "Export Logs"` (windows.ts:276, windows.ts:282). If buttons are localized, these string comparisons always fail. Must use button index (`result.response`) instead of label text.
repair_path: Replace string comparisons `button === "Export Logs"` with index comparison `result.response === 1` (or the appropriate index). The `handle` function already receives the button index indirectly through the button array position.

finding: Fix E: IPC locale race on renderer crash. The recovery dialog fires via `render-process-gone`, `did-fail-load`, and `unresponsive` events (windows.ts:335-373). At that point, the renderer process may be dead or hung, so `ipcMain.handle("get-locale-preference", () => storedLocale)` cannot fetch the active locale. The plan mentions 'proactive sync of locale to main process' only as 'fall back to English', but the code sketch doesn't show any proactive sync mechanism. The dialog will always show English buttons for crash scenarios regardless of user locale.
repair_path: Store the renderer's active locale in main-process memory (electron-store or a module variable) before any crash occurs. Add an IPC handler on the renderer side (e.g., `ipcRenderer.invoke("set-locale-preference", locale)`) that the language context calls on locale switch and on mount. Then `get-locale-preference` reads this stored value.

finding: Fix C: `tPlural` references free variable `dict` that isn't defined. The code in plural.ts shows: `return t(localeKey in dict ? localeKey : fallback, { count })`. `dict` is not imported or passed as a parameter — it's undefined at runtime.
repair_path: Remove the `localeKey in dict` check. The `t()` translator function already handles missing keys gracefully (returns the key name). Simplify to: `return t(localeKey, { count })` or pass a dictionary reference if key existence check is truly needed.

finding: Fix E: hardcoded English fallback strings in DIALOG_STRINGS (dialog-strings.ts) are not actually translated. The dialog-strings.ts module simply re-exports the English strings. No actual translation mechanism is shown — the plan only fills locale files in `packages/app/src/i18n/`, but dialog-strings lives in `packages/desktop/src/main/` and doesn't import from the app locale files. There's no path that connects the rendered locale dict to native dialog strings.
repair_path: Either (a) generate dialog-strings.ts from the same locale source so translations exist, or (b) establish a shared locale key set for dialog strings that lives in the desktop package and is consumed by both dialog-strings.ts and the app i18n layer, or (c) explicitly scope Fix E to English-only for this phase and file deferred follow-up for actual translation.

finding: Fix F: `updateTitlebar` uses `win.setTitleBarOverlay()` which throws on Linux compositors that don't support it. The plan mentions 'guard with try-catch' but the code sketch only changes the platform condition. Without a try-catch, any Linux user on an unsupported compositor (e.g., older GNOME, some Wayland implementations) will see a crash on titlebar update.
repair_path: Wrap `win.setTitleBarOverlay(overlay(...))` in `updateTitlebar` with try-catch that logs a warning. Also add try-catch around the `titleBarOverlay` constructor option in `createMainWindow` and `createLoadingWindow`, or validate support via `BrowserWindow.isSetTitleBarOverlaySupported()` if available in the Electron version.

finding: Fix B: The proposed parity.test.ts removes the existing assertion that non-English locales have DIFFERENT translations than English (`expect(locale[key]).not.toBe(en[key])`). Combined with Fix A filling gaps with English text + TODO comments, the test will pass even though all 18 locales contain English-only strings. The plan claims 'parity' but only checks key existence, not actual translation quality.
repair_path: Either (a) retain the `expect(locale[key]).not.toBe(en[key])` assertion but exclude keys with TODO comments, or (b) file a follow-up to separately track 'keys with English text' from 'translated keys' and add a test that fails when a certain threshold of untranslated keys is exceeded, or (c) explicitly document in the test that it only validates structural parity, not translation quality.

finding: The plan conflates 7 largely independent fixes (A: locale fill, B: test, C: pluralization, D: formatting, E: dialog strings, F: Linux titlebar, G: macOS vibrancy) into a single plan. Fixes A-D are app-side i18n, E is desktop IPC + dialogs, F-G are desktop platform UI. These could be 3 separate lanes: app-i18n-core, desktop-dialog-i18n, desktop-platform-ui. This split would improve reversibility and allow independent validation.
repair_path: Consider splitting into 3 separate plans: (1) App i18n parity + pluralization + formatting, (2) Desktop dialog i18n + IPC locale sync, (3) Linux titlebar + macOS vibrancy. Each can be independently validated and reverted.
